news.summary: A class-action lawsuit filed by BSV investors against Binance was dismissed by the court which refused to consider loss-of-chance claims. The ruling stated that damages based on loss of investment opportunities are not permissible under the law.
news.headline: UK court denies Bitcoin SV investors' $13.3B damages claim vs. Binance, citing lack of legal relevance for loss-of-chance claims.

news.summary: In Delray Beach, Florida, a recent report by MarketsandMarkets predicts that the Brake Friction Products Market will see significant growth, reaching a value of USD 15.52 billion by 2032, up from USD 13.32 billion in 2025, representing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 2.2%. As electric vehicles (EVs) are expected to claim a larger portion of the market in the future, this industry is likely to experience notable shifts.
news.headline: Brake Friction Products Market to grow to $15.52 billion by 2032 from $13.32 billion in 2025, at 2.2% CAGR.

news.summary: FIFA has chosen Avalanche to power its dedicated blockchain network for NFTs and fan engagement, catering to its over five billion global fans. This decision comes after FIFA's initial plan to launch a blockchain network for its collectibles. The new L1 blockchain will utilize Avalanche's scalability-focused infrastructure and AvaCloud's EVM compatibility for seamless integration with decentralized wallets and applications. Francesco Abbate, CEO of Modex and FIFA Collect, highlighted the speed, scalability, and EVM compatibility as key factors. FIFA will migrate its NFT marketplace and collection, FIFA Collect, to the Avalanche-powered blockchain. External Algorand-based wallets will no longer be supported post-migration, with users connecting via MetaMask or compatible EVM wallets. FIFA's NFT collection was launched ahead of the 2023 Club World Cup with modex. In November 2024, FIFA teamed up with Mythical Games for the FIFA Rivals soccer game for iOS and Android. The move aims to enhance fan experiences with unique digital collectibles and immersive features.
news.headline: FIFA chooses Avalanche for dedicated blockchain to power NFT platform, aiming to engage over 5 billion fans worldwide. Bitcoin at $109K high amid trade war relief.

news.summary: Titan Machinery Inc., a leading agricultural and construction equipment store network, reported its financial results for the fiscal first quarter ended April 30, 2025. The revenue was $594.3 million, a decrease from $628.7 million in the previous year's first quarter. Gross profit was $90.9 million, with a margin of 15.3%. Operating expenses were $96.4 million. Net loss for the quarter was $13.2 million. EBITDA was $2.6 million, down from $30.9 million in the previous year. The Agriculture segment saw revenue of $384.4 million. The Construction segment had revenue of $72.1 million, and the Europe and Australia segments also reported revenue. Cash at the end of the quarter was $21.5 million, with inventories at $1.1 billion. The company's net cash provided by operating activities was $6.2 million. Titan Machinery reiterated its full-year diluted adjusted earnings per share guidance and provided modeling assumptions for fiscal 2026. The company's forward-looking statements highlighted various risks and uncertainties impacting its operations. Investors were encouraged to review non-GAAP financial measures included in the release.
news.headline: Titan Machinery Inc. announced fiscal first quarter ended April 30, 2025 results: $594.3M revenue, $90.9M gross profit, $13.2M net loss. Agricultural segment revenue down 14.1%, construction segment pre-tax loss $4.2M, Europe segment revenue up 47.5%, pre-tax income $4.7M, Australia segment revenue up 3.6%. Cash at $21.5M, inventories at $1.1B. Operating expenses $96.4M. Net cash provided by operating activities was $6.2M. Titan Machinery Inc. operates in North America, Europe, and Australia. Management reiterates full year diluted adjusted earnings per share guidance. More details on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: The FDA has approved Purified Cortrophin Gel as the sole ACTH therapy for managing acute gout flares. This medication has been recognized by the FDA for its effectiveness in treating this condition.
news.headline: ANI Pharma starts Phase 4 trial for Cortrophin Gel in treating acute gout flares, the only FDA-approved ACTH therapy.

news.summary: In Hong Kong, Ant International has partnered with HSBC for the bank's new Tokenised Deposit Service. This collaboration aims to enhance treasury management by facilitating real-time, continuous HKD and USD payments between corporate wallets of a client at HSBC Hong Kong. The Tokenised Deposit Service represents the pioneering bank-led, blockchain settlement solution in Hong Kong. The initiative follows a prosperous trial period conducted between HSBC and Ant International.
news.headline: Ant International partners with HSBC to launch first bank-led, blockchain-based Tokenised Deposit Service in Hong Kong for real-time treasury management.

news.summary: Nathaniel Bradley, the CEO of Datavault AI, is set to address audiences at the upcoming Beyond Expo 2025 event in Beaverton, Oregon. The focus of his speech will be on Artificial Intelligence (AI) Agents and the Monetization of Name, Image, and Likeness (NIL) rights. This presentation showcases Bradley's expertise in these areas, highlighting the growing importance of AI and NIL strategies in the business world. The event promises to be an enriching experience, shedding light on innovative technologies and monetization opportunities in the industry.
news.headline: CEO Nathaniel Bradley of Datavault AI will discuss AI Agents and NIL Monetization at Beyond Expo 2025 in Beaverton.

news.summary: Options has expanded its presence in the Asia-Pacific region through support from InvestHK during its official launch in Hong Kong. This move reinforces Options' commitment to the APAC market, as it aims to capitalize on the growing demand for its services in the region. The backing from InvestHK signals confidence in Options' potential for growth and contribution to the local economy. This development comes as part of Options' global expansion strategy, leveraging its expertise to cater to the increasing number of clients in APAC. The investment from InvestHK will enable Options to further solidify its position in Hong Kong and boost its operations in the region.
news.headline: Options expands in APAC with support from InvestHK at Hong Kong launch. Updates from Hong Kong, London & New York.

news.summary: TELUS Digital Experience recently held its inaugural TELUS Days of Giving (TDOG) volunteer events on May 16-17. The leading global technology company, traded as TIXT on NYSE and TSX, is dedicated to enhancing digital customer experiences. With operations worldwide, TELUS Digital conducted numerous TDOG and volunteer events previously. Notably, this marked the first time the company's team members from regions like Canada, China, and E came together in a synchronized effort for this initiative.
news.headline: TELUS Digital teams globally join forces for synchronized volunteer events on May 16-17, organizing hundreds of TELUS Days of Giving (TDOG) globally, involving team members from Canada, China, E.

news.summary: Digital compression testing machines are crucial for determining the compressive strength of materials like concrete, metals, ceramics, and composites. Market growth is being driven by factors such as safety emphasis, quality control, and infrastructure development. The market was valued at USD 520 million in 2023, with a projected size of USD 1.23 billion by 2034, showcasing a CAGR of 8.1% from 2024 to 2034. These machines are crucial for industries like construction, manufacturing, and research due to their efficiency and accuracy. The integration of features like automation and real-time data collection has streamlined testing processes and improved quality control. AI is revolutionizing compression testing, enhancing efficiency and precision. Market dynamics are shaped by technological progress, advanced manufacturing needs, stricter regulatory standards, and cost-cutting pressures. Key growth drivers include the need for precise testing solutions and regulatory compliance, while market restraints include high initial costs and the need for skilled personnel. Opportunities lie in automation, sustainability trends, emerging markets, and AI integration. The market is segmented by product type (static and dynamic) and application (construction, material testing, aerospace, and automotive). Static machines are widely used for compressive tests in construction, while dynamic machines are increasingly utilized in R&D settings. Key applications include increased infrastructure projects in construction, evaluation of new materials in material testing, high-performance standards in aerospace, and precision testing needs in automotive.
news.headline: Digital compression testing machines are in demand due to safety, quality control, and infrastructure development trends. Market size in 2023 was USD 520 million, projected to rise to USD 1.23 billion in 2034 with a CAGR of 8.1%. AI integration, regulatory compliance, and industrial automation are driving market growth, despite high initial costs and the need for skilled personnel. Opportunities lie in automation, sustainability trends, emerging markets, and AI integration. Market segmented by product type (static, dynamic) and application (construction, material testing, aerospace, automotive).

news.summary: An important achievement has been reached for Brazil's biggest potash project, marking a significant step towards producing potash within the country for the agriculture sector. This development is crucial for meeting the growing demand for potash in Brazil and reducing dependence on imports. This milestone underscores the country's progress in enhancing its self-sufficiency in potash production and strengthening its agriculture industry.
news.headline: Site preparation work completed at future port terminal for Autazes Project in Brazil, critical for domestic potash production.

news.summary: Bitcoin's market cap surpassed that of Amazon on "Bitcoin Pizza Day," reaching $2.205 trillion compared to Amazon's $2.135 trillion. This milestone has attracted more attention from non-crypto audiences, with new rallies strengthening confidence. Bitcoin hit a new high above $110,000, attracting new investors and institutional players, with BlackRock becoming the second-largest Bitcoin holder. The total cryptocurrency market cap was $3.49 trillion, with Bitcoin ETFs receiving $604 million in net inflows. Projections see Bitcoin reaching $150,000 and $90,000 this year. Bitcoin's evolution from speculation to a serious asset class was noted on the anniversary of the first Bitcoin purchase in 2010. Bitcoin is now the fifth-largest global asset, showcasing a market capitalization exceeding $2 trillion.
news.headline: Bitcoin surpasses Amazon's market cap on 'Pizza Day'. BTC at $2.205 trillion, Amazon at $2.135 trillion. Bitcoin sets new high above $110,000. Total crypto market cap at $3.49 trillion. Bitcoin ETFs see $604 million inflows. Bitcoin expected to reach $200,000 with gradual adjustments. Today marks 'Bitcoin Pizza Day,' celebrating the first BTC purchase in 2010. Bitcoin market cap exceeds $2 trillion, making it fifth-largest asset globally.

news.summary: Financial expert Dave Ramsey criticized Bitcoin investors as "foolish," warning of impending losses in a radio segment from February 2014, when Bitcoin was valued at $549.26 per coin. Over a decade later, Bitcoin soared to a new all-time high of over $111,000 on Thursday, May 22, 2025, attributed to increased U.S. regulatory support and significant inflows into exchange-traded funds. This overnight surge past the crucial $110,000 level propelled Bitcoin to reach $111,861 by midday in New York, surpassing the previous high of $109,241 from Donald Trump's inauguration day. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bitcoin rose from $549.26 in 2014 to $111,861 in 2025, gaining momentum with U.S. regulatory changes and ETF inflows.

news.summary: In Toronto today, Granite Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX: GRT.UN / NYSE: GRP.U) shared that the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) has approved Granite's Notice of Intention to Make a Normal Course Issuer Bid (NCIB). Under the NCIB, Granite plans to buy up to 6,060 of its units within the next year through the TSX and other Canadian trading systems if deemed appropriate.
news.headline: Granite REIT renews its Normal Course Issuer Bid to buy up to 6,060 shares over the next 12 months.

news.summary: In Milton Keynes, England, Teletrac Navman, a prominent connected mobility platform under Vontier, has released the Mobilizing the Future of Fleets Report: 2025 Energy Edition. The report reveals that 63% of fleets attribute customer demand as a significant factor influencing their switch to alternative energies and the pace at which they make this transition. Furthermore, over half (58%) of surveyed businesses identify their brand reputation and sustainability objectives as key drivers in this shift.
news.headline: Teletrac Navman report reveals 63% of fleets cite customer demand for energy transition; 58% prioritize brand reputation/sustainability.
